name: Orifice Plate Flow Meter Sizing — Beta Ratio, Discharge Coefficient & Differential Pressure
subtitle: Instrumentation | Flow Measurement — ISO 5167-1:2022 §6, ISO 5167-2:2022 (orifice plates)

inScope: Orifice Plate Flow Meter Sizing — Beta Ratio, Discharge Coefficient & Differential Pressure
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Design (Maximum) Volume Flow Rate at Operating Conditions (Q_op)
Fluid Dynamic Viscosity at Operating Conditions (μ)

enum.label: Corner taps — ISO 5167 standard, most common
Flange taps (1 inch from faces) — ASME / AGA 3

inputs.label: Gas Isentropic Exponent (κ) - Set 1.0 for liquid

standards.name: Orifice Plate Flow Meter Sizing — Beta Ratio, Discharge Coefficient & Differential Pressure
standards.description: Orifice Plate Flow Meter Sizing — Beta Ratio, Discharge Coefficient & Differential Pressure — Instrumentation, Flow Measurement

formulas.expression: Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(pipe_ID_mm / 1000, 2); v_pipe_m_s = design_flow_m3h / 3600 / A_pipe_m2; Re_D = fluid_density_kg_m3 * v_pipe_m_s * pipe_ID_mm / 1000 / (fluid_viscosity_mPas / 1000)
formulas.reference: ISO 5167-1:2022 §6.1 — pipe Reynolds number determines discharge coefficient validity

formulas.expression: 0.6; epsilon = (isentropic_exponent > 1.0) ? 1 - 0.41 * Math.pow(0.5, 1/isentropic_exponent) : 1.0; beta = Math.pow(1 / (1 + Math.pow(Cd_est * epsilon * A_pipe_m2 * Math.sqrt(2 * max_dP_kpa * 1000 / fluid_density_kg_m3) / (design_flow_m3h / 3600), 2)), 0.25)
formulas.reference: ISO 5167-2 §5 — iterate β from Q = Cd·ε·(π/4)·d²·√(2ΔP/ρ·(1−β⁴)^−0.5)

formulas.expression: Math.pow(beta, 4); A_coef = (19000 * beta / Re_D) < 1 ? 0 : 0.0188 + 0.0063 * Math.pow(19000 * beta / Re_D, 0.8); Cd = 0.5961 + 0.0261 * beta * beta - 0.216 * Math.pow(beta, 8) + 0.000521 * Math.pow(1e6 * beta / Re_D, 0.7) + (0.0188 + 0.0063 * A_coef) * Math.pow(beta, 3.5) * Math.pow(1e6 / Re_D, 0.3)
formulas.reference: ISO 5167-2:2022 Eq.(1) — Reader-Harris/Gallagher correlation for corner taps

formulas.expression: (isentropic_exponent > 1.0) ? 1 - (0.351 + 0.256 * Math.pow(beta, 4) + 0.93 * Math.pow(beta, 8)) * (1 - Math.pow(1 - max_dP_kpa * 1000 / (fluid_density_kg_m3 * v_pipe_m_s * v_pipe_m_s / 2 * 100), 1/isentropic_exponent)) : 1.0; dP_actual_kpa = Math.pow(design_flow_m3h / 3600 / (Cd * epsilon_act * A_pipe_m2 * Math.pow(beta, 2) / Math.sqrt(1 - Math.pow(beta, 4))), 2) * fluid_density_kg_m3 / 2 / 1000
formulas.reference: ISO 5167-2 Eq.(4) — rearranged for ΔP given Q, Cd, β, ε

formulas.expression: 1 / Math.sqrt(1 - Math.pow(beta, 4)); dP_perm_kpa = dP_actual_kpa * (1 - Math.pow(beta, 2)) * (1 - Cd * Math.pow(beta, 2) * E_factor)
formulas.reference: ISO 5167-2 §5.1.7 — permanent pressure loss: ΔP_perm = ΔP·(1−β²)·(1−Cd·β²·E)

warnings.message: β > 0.65: high beta ratio — discharge coefficient uncertainty increases; upstream straight pipe requirement extends to 44D (vs 10D at β=0.3); verify straight run length
High gas velocity (>30 m/s): compressibility and expansibility correction significant — verify ε factor is applied; choked flow check required if ΔP/P > 0.03
Actual ΔP exceeds transmitter range: flow measurement will saturate — increase beta ratio or specify higher-range transmitter
High viscosity fluid (>50 mPa·s): orifice plate accuracy degrades for viscous fluids — consider Coriolis or gear meter for μ > 100 mPa·s
Low Re with high β: Cd correction for low-Re is significant — apply RHG equation iteratively; 3–5 iterations typically required for convergence to ±0.1%

verdictText.warn: PASS — High Utilization: Peer Review Required

legalNotice: ⚠ Engineering decision support only — verify before use. This is a technical simulation, not professional engineering advice.
